name: Asparagus, Leek, and Potato Soup
recipeIngredient: Leeks: 3 large, chopped (use white part and only 1 inch of green part)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare leeks by using the white part and only 1 inch of the green part.
2. Make parsley butter: Mix parsley, butter, salt, and pepper in a small bowl. This can be prepared up to 2 days ahead, covered, and refrigerated. Bring to room temperature before use.
3. Melt 1/4 cup unsalted butter in a heavy large saucepan over low heat.
4. Add leeks, thyme, and bay leaf to the pan. Cover and cook until leeks are soft, stirring occasionally about 10 minutes.
5. Mix in chicken stock/canned broth and potatoes. Bring to boil.
6. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until potatoes are tender, about 12 minutes.
7. Add asparagus and simmer until crisp-tender, about 3 minutes.
8. Discard bay leaf and season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Ladle soup into bowls and top each with a dollop of parsley butter.
This recipe sounded very good. My husband likes cream soups, so I added sweet onion to the potato mixture and pureed all before adding asparagus to the creamy soup. Adapted from Bon Appetit, April 1991. From the MM database of Judi M. Phelps (jphelps@shell.portal.com or jphelps@best.com).
